insight: Master Bot's skill varies hugely depending on the unit set in play; on some sets it plays reasonably well, but on others it effectively throws the game. This makes its difficulty highly inconsistent across games.
author: consensus
context: Discussion about how hard it is for new players to reach 50% winrate against Master Bot, with sano712 and velizar_ noting the bot's performance varies wildly by set.

insight: Master Bot can be exploited by offering a gambit that any human would accept but the bot declines, or by setting up a large purchase that only works if the AI buys a Wall instead of a Steelsplitter — which it reliably does even when a Steelsplitter would block the attack anyway.
author: masn6811
context: Discussion about whether a heuristic-heavy bot would be useful, with masn6811 citing specific known exploitable patterns in the existing AI.

insight: When using the absorb command (Q absorb), the bot soaks damage onto Xae rather than absorbing on Chieftain, because Chieftain has more HP and does not fit the standard soak heuristic. Elyot confirmed the heuristics do not handle Chieftain correctly and added it to the bug list.
author: silentslayers
context: silentslayers reported the absorb/soak misbehavior on Chieftain twice, and elyot confirmed it is a known bug due to heuristic limitations.

insight: Community members and velizar_ specifically suggested creating a curated set of units that Master Bot plays well with, so that players could optionally generate games using only those units to get a more consistent and high-quality bot opponent.
author: velizar_
context: Discussion about Master Bot's highly variable performance across different unit sets, with velizar_ proposing a filtered set as a workaround.

insight: According to a 2015 academic paper on the Prismata AI, Master Bot played at approximately the top 25% of human players at the time of writing. However, velizar_ estimated that by 2020 it was likely only in the top 85% of current active players, suggesting the human playerbase has improved relative to the bot.
author: velizar_
context: Players discussing a cited statistic from the PrismataAI academic paper about bot strength relative to the human ladder.

insight: The Fragile + Frontline combination is a deliberately avoided interaction in Prismata's design. Prompt + Lifespan combined (as seen on Doomed Wall) has always been intentional and was present from the game's earliest days.
author: elyot
context: masn6811 asked about why Innervi Field did not combine Prompt and Lifespan; elyot clarified the design history of combined keyword interactions.

insight: Elyot confirmed that more units in a set (e.g. B+8 vs B+5) creates more opportunities to make marginally better plays, which in theory should affect the k-factor (variance) of rating points but not the rating itself. The current rating system does not adjust for this.
author: elyot
context: Discussion about whether B+5 games are less skill-differentiating than B+8 games and whether the rating system should reflect that.

insight: Oxide Mixer is deliberately kept at low supply because units that produce other units can cause resource piles to become unmanageably large at high supply counts. The same design reasoning applies to Thermite.
author: elyot
context: velizar_ speculated about the low supply of Oxide Mixer; elyot confirmed the rationale is to prevent pile overflow for unit-producing units.
